releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.15
releases.excerpt: A new onboarding flow helps you get to your first working setup faster. First-run guidance is more structured and does a better job walking you through permissions, setup, and template creation.
highlights: A new onboarding flow helps you get to your first working setup faster. First-run guidance is more structured and does a better job walking you through permissions, setup, and template creation.
Build custom prompts and templates in chat. You can now create and refine naming setups through a more conversational chat-first flow instead of configuring everything manually upfront.
LM Studio models can be loaded in-app. Browse, load, and manage LM Studio models without leaving NameQuick.
File dates are available as template fallback values. Use created, modified, or accessed dates when a document is missing stronger metadata.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.14
releases.excerpt: A new onboarding flow helps you get to your first working setup faster. First-run guidance is more structured and does a better job walking you through permissions, setup, and template creation.
highlights: A new onboarding flow helps you get to your first working setup faster. First-run guidance is more structured and does a better job walking you through permissions, setup, and template creation.
Build custom prompts and templates in chat. You can now create and refine naming setups through a more conversational chat-first flow instead of configuring everything manually upfront.
LM Studio models can be loaded in-app. Browse, load, and manage LM Studio models without leaving NameQuick.
File dates are available as template fallback values. Use created, modified, or accessed dates when a document is missing stronger metadata.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.13
releases.excerpt: Metadata results are now more consistent across AI providers. Filename, tags, and description generation now behaves more predictably when you switch between supported providers.
highlights: Metadata results are now more consistent across AI providers. Filename, tags, and description generation now behaves more predictably when you switch between supported providers.
The update banner now includes a full "What's New" view. You can expand details in-app, review highlights, and choose to install now or be reminded later.
Metadata handling is more reliable when AI responses are incomplete. NameQuick now recovers more often instead of failing, and continues with filename-only output when needed.
Template placeholders are more reliable across languages and regions. Date placeholders are more stable, and field matching now handles accented characters more accurately.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.12
releases.excerpt: OpenRouter is now available as a BYOK provider. Bring your own OpenRouter API key to access hundreds of models from a single provider.
highlights: OpenRouter is now available as a BYOK provider. Bring your own OpenRouter API key to access hundreds of models from a single provider.
Presets sort alphabetically. The Presets list (formerly "Custom Prompts") now sorts A–Z by default for easier browsing.
Japanese and CJK filenames no longer get mangled. Smart rename no longer inserts separators between every character in CJK output.
Expired BYOK trials stay usable. An expired trial no longer hard-blocks the app — you can still browse files and explore before upgrading.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.11
releases.excerpt: BYOK trial email login works reliably. We now recognize trial entitlements even without an active subscription, so your BYOK trial email won’t get rejected.
highlights: BYOK trial email login works reliably. We now recognize trial entitlements even without an active subscription, so your BYOK trial email won’t get rejected.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.10
releases.excerpt: BYOK license no longer lost after Keychain hiccups. Temporary Keychain access issues no longer clear your saved API keys.
highlights: BYOK license no longer lost after Keychain hiccups. Temporary Keychain access issues no longer clear your saved API keys.
Offline startup works with newer subscription plans. No longer requires an internet connection on every launch.
Onboarding shows accurate key status. Setup flow correctly reflects when keys are actually usable.
File list updates more reliably. Changes to tags and metadata now refresh immediately.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.09
releases.excerpt: Per-field required/optional settings. Each extraction field now has its own required/optional dropdown, replacing the global toggle. This gives you precise control over which fields must have values for a rename to proceed.
highlights: Per-field required/optional settings. Each extraction field now has its own required/optional dropdown, replacing the global toggle. This gives you precise control over which fields must have values for a rename to proceed.
In-app update notifications. A banner now appears when an update is available, with quick access to install or view the changelog.

Redesigned AI provider cards. Provider cards now use accordion behavior—expanding one collapses others. Selection is integrated directly into each card, and the app auto-selects a valid provider when you save a new API key.
More reliable watch folder processing. Files added during brief monitoring pauses are now caught by a follow-up scan.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.07
releases.excerpt: German language support. The app is now fully localized in German. Language follows your system preference. Feedback on the translations is welcome—reach out via email.
highlights: German language support. The app is now fully localized in German. Language follows your system preference. Feedback on the translations is welcome—reach out via email.

Credit tracking is more reliable across app restarts. Fixed an issue where credit usage could be lost or reset unexpectedly when the app restarted during a billing period. Your usage now persists correctly even if sync timing varies.
Monthly credit rollover timing is more accurate. Improved scheduling so credit allowances reset at the correct time when a new billing period begins.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.06
releases.excerpt: Local text-only models now work with text PDFs. Ollama and LM Studio text-only models can process PDFs that contain extractable text. Clear error messages appear when a vision model is needed for images or scanned PDFs.
highlights: Local text-only models now work with text PDFs. Ollama and LM Studio text-only models can process PDFs that contain extractable text. Clear error messages appear when a vision model is needed for images or scanned PDFs.
Quick access to support email. Help menu and Settings now include direct links to reach support.
SVG files process correctly for subscription users. Fixed an issue where SVG, AI, and EPS files failed when processed through the subscription path.
Rules action editors stay visible. Tag and comment action editors no longer disappear unexpectedly. Archive folder picker now correctly shows the base folder with relative path.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.05
releases.excerpt: Subscription credits now display correctly. Fixed an issue where credits showed as 0 for subscription customers despite having available balance.
highlights: Subscription credits now display correctly. Fixed an issue where credits showed as 0 for subscription customers despite having available balance.
Shift-click selection works reliably. File table selection range now behaves correctly when shift-clicking.
Fixed memory leaks during inference. Resolved memory growth when processing many files.
More reliable watch folder processing.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.04
releases.excerpt: Subscription credits now display correctly. Fixed an issue where some subscription customers saw 0 credits available despite having an active plan.
highlights: Subscription credits now display correctly. Fixed an issue where some subscription customers saw 0 credits available despite having an active plan.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.03
releases.excerpt: Local model downloads in Settings – Local Providers now list recommended Ollama and LM Studio models with hardware guidance and quick download or copy actions.
highlights: Local model downloads in Settings – Local Providers now list recommended Ollama and LM Studio models with hardware guidance and quick download or copy actions.
More reliable local model output – Local prompts now enforce template rules so assembled filenames match transformed fields, and JSON/fenced responses are cleaned automatically.
Cleaner freeform naming on local providers – Ollama and LM Studio freeform authoring now uses chat endpoints for more consistent responses.
Suffixes now stick with dot separators. Smart Rename no longer drops suffix text when you use . as the separator.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.01
releases.excerpt: Instant Search – Press ⌘F to search across all settings. Select a result to jump directly to that setting, highlighted and ready to adjust.
highlights: Instant Search – Press ⌘F to search across all settings. Select a result to jump directly to that setting, highlighted and ready to adjust.
Keyboard Navigation – Use ⌘1 through ⌘6 to jump to any category instantly.
Refreshed AI Provider Cards – Each provider displays its official logo with clear connection status indicators.
More List Columns – Six new column options: Last Viewed, Created Date, Modified Date, File Size, Tags, and Comments. Choose up to 3 to display.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.11.00
releases.excerpt: Try NameQuick Free with Your Own API Key – New users can now try NameQuick free for 14 days using their own OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, or Ollama API key. The trial includes a set number of AI-powered renames to help you evaluate the app before committing.
highlights: Try NameQuick Free with Your Own API Key – New users can now try NameQuick free for 14 days using their own OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, or Ollama API key. The trial includes a set number of AI-powered renames to help you evaluate the app before committing.

Subscription Trials for New Customers – If you prefer not to use your own API key, you can now start a free trial of our managed AI service. Existing customers returning after a break will see a welcome-back flow that recognizes their account.

Faster, More Memory-Efficient Image Processing – Renamed files containing images now process more efficiently with lower memory usage. Large batches of screenshots and photos should no longer cause slowdowns or high memory consumption.

More Reliable Gemini Integration – Files that Gemini cannot process (due to content restrictions) now display a clear error message instead of retrying silently. Credits are only charged when a rename actually succeeds.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.10.09
releases.excerpt: - Fixed an issue where template-based renaming could produce generic filenames like _.pdf instead of extracting meaningful names from your documents. Templates now reliably capture the fields you've defined.
highlights: - Fixed an issue where template-based renaming could produce generic filenames like _.pdf instead of extracting meaningful names from your documents. Templates now reliably capture the fields you've defined.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.10.08
releases.excerpt: - Fixed a rare issue where subscription credits could stay “stuck” on a device after your billing period renewed or your plan changed.
highlights: - Fixed a rare issue where subscription credits could stay “stuck” on a device after your billing period renewed or your plan changed.

- Fixed an issue where custom prompts that analyze screenshots or images would sometimes ignore the visual content and only consider text. These prompts now correctly take the image itself into account when generating results.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.10.07
releases.excerpt: Added sorting options to the toolbar. You can now sort files by name, date, size, or type in ascending or descending order.
highlights: Added sorting options to the toolbar. You can now sort files by name, date, size, or type in ascending or descending order.

Fixed an issue where custom separators in naming patterns (like dashes, underscores, or spaces) could be lost when reopening or duplicating a pattern. Your patterns now preserve exactly the separators you chose.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.10.06
releases.excerpt: Fixed custom separators with spaces (like " - ") being stripped down to just the character (e.g., "-"). Filenames now correctly preserve space-surrounded separators for formats like "2024-01-15 - Invoice.pdf".
highlights: Fixed custom separators with spaces (like " - ") being stripped down to just the character (e.g., "-"). Filenames now correctly preserve space-surrounded separators for formats like "2024-01-15 - Invoice.pdf".
Fixed filename patterns dropping custom literal text. Any static words you add (e.g., "Test") now stay in the assembled filename alongside your extracted fields.
Fixed template testing failures caused by overly strict validation of AI response audit logs. Processing now correctly succeeds when all required fields are extracted.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.10.05
releases.excerpt: Fixed issue where duplicate files could incorrectly receive the same AI-generated name as a previously processed file.
highlights: Fixed issue where duplicate files could incorrectly receive the same AI-generated name as a previously processed file.
Fixed template preview incorrectly showing "Could not extract" errors when fields were actually extracted successfully.
Fixed sidebar disappearing after closing Settings.
Added tooltips to sidebar tabs explaining their purpose (All Files, To Process, Processed).

releases.title: NameQuick 2.10.04
releases.excerpt: Improved reliability for managed AI processing.
highlights: Improved reliability for managed AI processing.
Added recovery handling for corrupted template files with a clear warning when detected.
Fixed issue where file renames could appear successful without actually renaming the file.
Fixed processing delay setting not saving between sessions.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.10.03
releases.excerpt: Fixed an issue where auto-processing with Custom Prompts would ignore document content, resulting in poor rename quality.
highlights: Fixed an issue where auto-processing with Custom Prompts would ignore document content, resulting in poor rename quality.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.10.02
releases.excerpt: Fixed a bug where Custom Prompts created in earlier versions weren't visible after updating to v2.10.00 or v2.10.01. Your prompts are automatically restored on launch.
highlights: Fixed a bug where Custom Prompts created in earlier versions weren't visible after updating to v2.10.00 or v2.10.01. Your prompts are automatically restored on launch.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.10.01
releases.excerpt: Fixed an issue where some users were shown the onboarding flow again after restarting the app, even though they had already completed it.
highlights: Fixed an issue where some users were shown the onboarding flow again after restarting the app, even though they had already completed it.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.10.00
releases.excerpt: Visual pattern builder with drag-and-drop chips that show exactly how your filename will look
highlights: Visual pattern builder with drag-and-drop chips that show exactly how your filename will look
Per-field separators so you can mix dashes, underscores, and spaces however you need
Live preview updates as you build, so you always know what you're getting
Test your templates while building and iterate until they're perfect

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.47
releases.excerpt: Another small bug fix for subscription users.
highlights: Another small bug fix for subscription users.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.46
releases.excerpt: Small bug fix for subscription users.
highlights: Small bug fix for subscription users.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.45
releases.excerpt: Fixed a license logging crash when disk space is low by serializing writes on an actor and gating the HUD to foreground state.
highlights: Fixed a license logging crash when disk space is low by serializing writes on an actor and gating the HUD to foreground state.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.44
releases.excerpt: Credits now properly refresh when your subscription billing cycle renews. Previously, some users saw their old credit balance until they logged out and back in.
highlights: Credits now properly refresh when your subscription billing cycle renews. Previously, some users saw their old credit balance until they logged out and back in.
Cached credit data is now automatically invalidated when a new billing period begins, ensuring you always see your current balance.
Large PDFs now process more reliably. Increased internal compression target from 15 MB to 45 MB, allowing high-resolution scanned documents to succeed where they previously failed.
Oversized PDFs now show a clear error message instead of failing silently. If a PDF exceeds the 50 MB processing limit, you'll see exactly what the problem is and how to fix it.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.43
releases.excerpt: NameQuick 2.0.43 —
highlights: NameQuick 2.0.43 —
Smoother BYOK licensing
What’s fixed - Removes the “license validation” warning HUD that
could briefly appear on launch for customers using BYOK/Local license

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.42
releases.excerpt: NameQuick
highlights: NameQuick
2.0.42 — Reliable renames on macOS 26.1
What’s fixed - Fixed a rare issue where some files couldn’t be opened
or renamed after using NameQuick on macOS 26.1. - Files renamed with

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.41
releases.excerpt: Small fixes and improvements
highlights: Small fixes and improvements

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.40
releases.excerpt: Fix: resolves a rare freeze on macOS Sonoma where the sidebar repeatedly hid and showed, spiking CPU and stalling the app. NameQuick now opens and stays responsive.
highlights: Fix: resolves a rare freeze on macOS Sonoma where the sidebar repeatedly hid and showed, spiking CPU and stalling the app. NameQuick now opens and stays responsive.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.39
releases.excerpt: Smarter duplicate-name handling: when a target filename already exists, NameQuick now automatically appends a counter (e.g., ” (2)“) and completes the rename, instead of showing a”file already exists” error. This keeps your flow uninterrupted and preserves the original extension.
highlights: Smarter duplicate-name handling: when a target filename already exists, NameQuick now automatically appends a counter (e.g., ” (2)“) and completes the rename, instead of showing a”file already exists” error. This keeps your flow uninterrupted and preserves the original extension.

Gemini model updates (current + compatible): the selector now shows the latest 2.5 and 2.0 language models that work with NameQuick’s generateContent flow, and removes legacy/unsupported entries. Added: Gemini 2.5 Pro, 2.5 Flash, 2.5 Flash‑Lite, 2.5 Flash‑Lite (Preview 09‑2025); Gemini 2.0 Flash, 2.0 Flash‑Lite, 2.0 Flash (Experimental).
Removed: Gemini 1.5/1.0 families, Live‑only, and TTS models (not compatible with this flow).
Automatic migration: if you previously selected a removed model (e.g., 1.5/1.0, Live preview, old Flash‑Lite preview ID), you’ll switch to the closest 2.5 equivalent on launch and in the selector.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.38
releases.excerpt: Implemented a bug fix focusing on size limitations experienced by a user
highlights: Implemented a bug fix focusing on size limitations experienced by a user
Some crash fixes

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.37
releases.excerpt: Large PDF uploads are now processed reliably without memory spikes. We preprocess PDFs before reading them, keeping memory use low and preventing crashes on very large files.
highlights: Large PDF uploads are now processed reliably without memory spikes. We preprocess PDFs before reading them, keeping memory use low and preventing crashes on very large files.
Fixed vertical overflow issues in the Settings view for improved layout and accessibility
General stability improvements and crash fixes

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.36
releases.excerpt: Drag to reorder folders in the sidebar
highlights: Drag to reorder folders in the sidebar
Exclude Subfolders now sticks reliably
Re-adding removed folders works immediately (no extra permission steps)
Better filename fidelity and control Parentheses and square brackets are now preserved in names (e.g., “(Company)”, “[25 AUG ’25]”).

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.35
releases.excerpt: Per‑folder subfolder control: include or exclude subfolders for each watch folder from the sidebar context menu.
highlights: Per‑folder subfolder control: include or exclude subfolders for each watch folder from the sidebar context menu.
Right‑click a watch folder in the sidebar → toggle “Include Subfolders”.
Fixed an issue where custom prompts did not run when dragging and dropping a file into the app in subscription mode.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.34
releases.excerpt: Added a global Clean filenames toggle under Preferences → General & Permissions → Defaults. When enabled, NameQuick transliterates German umlauts (ä→ae, ö→oe, ü→ue, Ä→Ae, Ö→Oe, Ü→Ue, ß→ss) and keeps only letters, numbers, single spaces, underscores, and dashes across Smart Rename, Templates, and Custom Prompt flows.
highlights: Added a global Clean filenames toggle under Preferences → General & Permissions → Defaults. When enabled, NameQuick transliterates German umlauts (ä→ae, ö→oe, ü→ue, Ä→Ae, Ö→Oe, Ü→Ue, ß→ss) and keeps only letters, numbers, single spaces, underscores, and dashes across Smart Rename, Templates, and Custom Prompt flows.
Updated the rename preview and documentation to call out the exact cleanup rules so teams know what stays and what goes before uploading files.

Replaced the credit usage queue with a GRDB-backed worker that keeps retries off the main thread, prevents runaway JSON re-encoding, and eliminates the high-CPU watchdog terminations some users saw when Polar was slow.
Removed the deprecated Smart Rename and Basic settings tabs now that the Craft-style sidebar covers those options.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.32
releases.excerpt: Drag & Drop and Global Shortcut : Both operations now consistently apply prefixes and suffixes. ### UI Layout Improvements
highlights: Drag & Drop and Global Shortcut : Both operations now consistently apply prefixes and suffixes. ### UI Layout Improvements
Settings Window Responsiveness : Fixed layout overflow issues at compact window widths.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.31
releases.excerpt: Claude BYOK pacing – Batch rename jobs automatically throttle requests so you can push more files without tripping Anthropic’s burst limits or seeing 429 errors.
highlights: Claude BYOK pacing – Batch rename jobs automatically throttle requests so you can push more files without tripping Anthropic’s burst limits or seeing 429 errors.

Managed prompt creation – Fixed a crash that occurred when Managed (Plug & Play) subscribers pressed “New Prompt.” The add/edit form now opens inline instead of stacking a second sheet, so the NSWindowAlreadyHasSheetException path is gone.

Watch folder stability – Eliminated duplicate watch-folder entries that caused _MergeError crashes at launch, especially on macOS Sequoia. Saved profiles are cleaned automatically and add/remove now handle path variants safely.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.30
releases.excerpt: Fixed false routing in subscription mode
highlights: Fixed false routing in subscription mode

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.29
releases.excerpt: License activation no longer crashes. If something goes wrong while activating, you’ll just see the warning message.
highlights: License activation no longer crashes. If something goes wrong while activating, you’ll just see the warning message.
Connectivity validation keeps running. Skipping a recent success no longer refreshes the cache, so periodic checks still execute and catch environment/token issues.
Credit balance sync now surfaces instantly on launch. Cached snapshots load immediately while Polar refresh runs in the background, eliminating the multi-second stall when reopening the app.
Reduced Polar auth chatter. Successful connectivity checks are cached for 10 minutes so relaunches skip the redundant HEAD request that previously blocked credit syncing.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.28
releases.excerpt: Custom prompts moved to Processing settings. Find “Manage Custom Prompts…” inside Settings → Processing ### Bug Fixes
highlights: Custom prompts moved to Processing settings. Find “Manage Custom Prompts…” inside Settings → Processing ### Bug Fixes
Crash on launch eliminated. The Accessibility relaunch prompt now always runs on the main actor, so fresh installs and permission changes no longer trip a Swift precondition and terminate the app.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.27
releases.excerpt: Quick actions stay visible even before you select files. The floating process bar no longer disappears when you load a batch, making it obvious how to kick off renames and when actions are disabled versus ready to run.
highlights: Quick actions stay visible even before you select files. The floating process bar no longer disappears when you load a batch, making it obvious how to kick off renames and when actions are disabled versus ready to run.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.26
releases.excerpt: Never fall back to BYOK for managed accounts. If we’re momentarily offline from Polar, NameQuick now keeps your Managed AI license active, works from cached access, and automatically returns to the managed Gemini service once reconnected.
highlights: Never fall back to BYOK for managed accounts. If we’re momentarily offline from Polar, NameQuick now keeps your Managed AI license active, works from cached access, and automatically returns to the managed Gemini service once reconnected.

Clear warning and logging when service is offline. You’ll see a brief “Managed AI is temporarily offline…” banner if we’re working from cached access, and the diagnostics log captures the fallback event so support can diagnose it quickly.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.25
releases.excerpt: Adaptive menu bar icon – The menu bar symbol now respects light and dark appearances, keeping contrast consistent even when the system theme switches mid-session.
highlights: Adaptive menu bar icon – The menu bar symbol now respects light and dark appearances, keeping contrast consistent even when the system theme switches mid-session.
Quickly hide the menu bar icon – Added a Show Menu Bar Icon toggle in Appearance and General settings so you can run NameQuick without the status-item when you prefer a cleaner menu bar.
Correct vision model command – Updated the “Add Vision Model” action in Settings so it now copies ollama run qwen2.5vl:32b , ensuring the suggested Ollama command works without manual correction.
No other changes in this build; this release focuses on menu bar polish and one Ollama setup fix.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.24
releases.excerpt: ## Features
highlights: ## Features
## Fixes

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.23
releases.excerpt: New queue management popover – Introducing a dedicated queue popover where you can view all your queued rename files. Remove individual items or clear the entire queue with a single click—everything updates instantly with live badge counts.
highlights: New queue management popover – Introducing a dedicated queue popover where you can view all your queued rename files. Remove individual items or clear the entire queue with a single click—everything updates instantly with live badge counts.
Editing made stable – Fixed an issue where the app would crash when trying to edit your default naming template. You can now safely customize all your templates without any hiccups.

License management simplified – Resolved a quirk where the app would sometimes re-activate itself after you’d reset your license. Once you unlink your license, it stays unlinked until you choose to activate it again.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.22
releases.excerpt: Redesigned for macOS Tahoe – Beautiful new user interface optimized for Apple’s latest macOS, with modern visual refinements throughout the app.
highlights: Redesigned for macOS Tahoe – Beautiful new user interface optimized for Apple’s latest macOS, with modern visual refinements throughout the app.
Vector artists rejoice – Drop in AI, SVG, or EPS files and NameQuick will import, preview, and rename them just like your photos.
Templates that feel native – The Templates screen now looks and behaves like Finder. Quick actions sit right where you expect them, and the pencil button jumps straight into editing.
Memory leak fixed – Eliminated a critical memory leak that could cause crashes during extended use—the app now runs reliably for hours without issues.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.21
releases.excerpt: Before Rename : Rules evaluate files as they enter watch folders, perfect for initial routing and organization
highlights: Before Rename : Rules evaluate files as they enter watch folders, perfect for initial routing and organization
After Rename : Rules can run again after AI completes renaming, allowing you to organize files based on their final names
{original} - Original filename without extension
{date_taken} - EXIF date taken or creation date

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.20
releases.excerpt: ## Features
highlights: ## Features
## Fixes

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.19
releases.excerpt: v2.0.19 — Create Your Own
highlights: v2.0.19 — Create Your Own
Your naming rules, automated.
You can now write custom prompts for how you want files renamed. Pick
one from the toolbar, and NameQuick will use it for every file until you

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.18
releases.excerpt: All‑new onboarding flow with just‑in‑time prompts and a faster path to your first rename.
highlights: All‑new onboarding flow with just‑in‑time prompts and a faster path to your first rename.
Undo renames from grid view, list view, and the Mac menu bar.
Cleaner Mac menu bar UI.
The processing spinner is now in the Mac menu bar.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.17
releases.excerpt: Email login: Addresses like name+tag@domain.com now work as expected.
highlights: Email login: Addresses like name+tag@domain.com now work as expected.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.16
releases.excerpt: No more login Keychain prompts when viewing AI settings.
highlights: No more login Keychain prompts when viewing AI settings.
Smoother settings experience with non‑interactive Keychain reads.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.15
releases.excerpt: Templates (Claude): Reliable structured output with consistent results.
highlights: Templates (Claude): Reliable structured output with consistent results.
OpenAI schema: Fixes rare “type: None” error in structured output.
Spreadsheets: Stable memory + faster previews for large XLSX files.
Error messages: Clearer, provider‑specific guidance.

releases.title: NameQuick 2.0.14
releases.excerpt: New: You can now rename Word, PowerPoint, and Excel files (DOCX, PPTX, XLSX) .
highlights: New: You can now rename Word, PowerPoint, and Excel files (DOCX, PPTX, XLSX) .
BYOK: Claude (Anthropic) joins the lineup with native PDF + image analysis and explicit model selection.
Word (.docx): Smart, clean names for your documents — fast previews included.
PowerPoint (.pptx): Uses slide text to suggest clear context-aware names.

releases.title: NameQuick 1.3.20
releases.excerpt: Various improvements and bug fixes
highlights: Various improvements and bug fixes

releases.title: NameQuick 1.3.11
releases.excerpt: Various improvements and bug fixes
highlights: Various improvements and bug fixes

highlights: Various improvements and bug fixes

releases.title: NameQuick 1.1.4
releases.excerpt: Various improvements and bug fixes
highlights: Various improvements and bug fixes
